question: Does the current repository provide positive evidence that the retained legacy surfaces have external/public consumers, or does the uncertainty remain unresolved?

legacy_demotions: features.py declares itself a legacy compatibility shim and points internal runtime to features_asof.
validator.py declares itself intentionally legacy/test-only and points runtime validation to ConfigAuthority.validate.
ARCHITECTURE.md says core.config.validator exposes only legacy/test-only helpers and is not runtime authority.
runtime_config_live_update_matrix lists core.config.validator helpers under Legacy helper surface.
ARCHITECTURE_VISUAL classifies core.strategy.features as DEPRECATED_PATH and core.config.validator as TEST_ONLY.
public_repo_guidance: README describes Genesis-Core as deterministic backtest/optimization plus a FastAPI service.
README quickstart points to uvicorn core.server:app and config/API usage, not legacy module imports.
src/core/server.py is the canonical application assembler/entrypoint.
package_surface: Editable install guidance exists, so the modules remain technically importable by external consumers.
repo_local_usage: test_no_legacy_feature_imports.py blocks internal repo imports of core.strategy.features.
test_dead_code_tripwires.py uses the legacy surfaces only for containment/delegation enforcement.
test_config_endpoints.py imports legacy validator helpers for explicit test-only compatibility coverage.

inferred.best_read: No positive repo-local external/public consumer evidence exists, but package importability keeps absence unproven.

unverified: Whether any private/local automation outside this repo imports core.strategy.features.
Whether any private/local automation outside this repo imports core.config.validator.
Whether any published wheel/editable-install consumers rely on these modules today.
Whether a future explicit package/public-API contract slice would narrow the uncertainty enough for stronger lifecycle action.

keep_now: V2 default exclusion for the retained legacy surfaces

what_changed: A bounded external-usage uncertainty artifact now exists for the retained legacy surfaces.
The distinction between no positive repo-local evidence and no possible external consumers is now explicit.
The current V2 exclusion rationale is better grounded.
what_did_not_change: No file was moved, archived, or deleted.
The locally modified V2 boundary artifact remained untouched.
The locally modified runtime surface classification artifact remained untouched.
